content.full_text:
CITY HALL • 250 EAST L STREET • BENICIA, CA 94510 • (707) 746-4200

JUN 11 [illegible] '92

June 8, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTENTION: Legal Division

SUBJECT: Request for Written Advice Pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b)

To Staff Counsel:

I am the City Attorney for the City of Benicia, and am writing on
behalf of a member of the Benicia City Council to request written
advice with respect to the Council member's duties under the Fair
Political Practices Act. The Council member has discussed the
issue with me and authorized this request. This request is made
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b). The specific issues
and material facts, as they have been related to me, are described
below:

SKY VALLEY BENICIA

The City of Benicia is currently processing a Specific Plan for an
approximately 5,000 acres area of land located in Solano County,
and commonly referred to as "Sky Valley." The plan area is almost
completely undeveloped. The land is currently used primarily for
cattle grazing and passive recreation activities. The plan area is
for the most part located outside Benicia's current city limits.
County zoning is exclusive agricultural requiring a minimum of 20
acres per parcel for development purposes.

The Specific Plan being considered by the city proposes the
development of a high quality residential community consisting of
5,360 homes. Ancillary uses proposed for the area include parks,
schools, a neighborhood shopping center, and large areas of open
space. The proposed plan also shows an 18-hole golf course to be
open for public use. A copy of a diagram of the proposed land use
plan is included for your reference.

The primary freeway access to the planned area is by Lake Herman
Road from its intersection with Interstate 680. If the plan is
implemented, Lake Herman Road will be widened from two lanes to
four lanes to accommodate the increased traffic flow.
Additionally, it will be necessary to expand and improve the on-
and off-ramps at the intersection of Lake Herman Road and I-680.

MARILYN CITRON O'ROURKE, Mayor                    MICHAEL WARREN, City Manager
Members of the City Council                        VIRGINIA SOUZA, City Treasurer
DIRK FULTON Mayor Pro Tem • JOHN P. SILVA • ERNEST F. CIARROCCHI • RICHARD W. SHAFER
                                                   FRANCES GRECO, City Clerk

                                    Recycled [symbol] Paper


Staff Counsel
Page 2
June 8, 1992


COUNCILMAN FULTON'S PROPERTY INTERESTS

Councilman Dirk Fulton and his wife own fee title to an undeveloped
1.82 acre parcel bounded by East Second Street, Lake Herman Road,
and Interstate 680. The property is located within the Benicia
city limits, and is zoned commercial general. This zoning allows
a wide variety of commercial uses. The property is encumbered by
a Cal Trans right-of-way along its Lake Herman Road frontage. A
Cal Trans encroachment permit would be required in order to gain
access to the parcel from Lake Herman Road. Such permits are
difficult to obtain and access to the parcel would likely be
obtained via Second Street.

The parcel is located more than 4,500 ft. from the nearest
boundaries of the Sky Valley Plan area. The property is well over
one mile from the closest area to be developed within the plan
area. There are well over ten parcels under separate ownership
within a 2,500 ft. radius of the Fulton parcel.

FUTURE CITY COUNCIL ACTIONS REQUIRED

In order to adopt and implement the Specific Plan, the City Council
will have to take several different actions. First, the Council
must certify an environmental impact report completed pursuant to
the requirements of the California Environmental Quality Act which
identifies and discusses the environmental impact of the proposed
plan. Then, it will be necessary for the City Council to adopt a
General Plan Amendment, since the city's current General Plan does
not envision the type and scope of development currently proposed
in the Sky Valley Specific Plan. Additionally, it is anticipated
that the City will enter into a development agreement with some or
all of the developers and property owners in the plan area. It
will be necessary for the Council to review and approve the
development agreement.

If approved and implemented, the Sky Valley Specific Plan will have
a profound effect on the City of Benicia. It would add several new
parks to the city's park system, miles of new roads, sewers, and
storm drains, and would increase the city's current population by
at least 40 percent. Councilman Fulton would most certainly like
to participate, to the greatest extent possible, in decisions that
will implement this plan, and bring such tremendous change to the
lives of his constituents. However, Councilman Fulton is cognizant
of the requirements of the Fair Political Practices Act, and does
not wish to breach his obligations under the law. Accordingly, I
request, on behalf of Councilman Fulton, that the Commission review
the facts as explained in this request, and provide Councilman
Fulton with written advice as to whether he may participate in any
or all of the planning actions the City Council will take on the


Staff Counsel
Page 3
June 8, 1992


proposed Sky Valley Specific Plan.  If you require additional
information with regards to this request, please feel free to
contact the undersigned at (707) 746-4216.

Your written response to this request may be sent to:

                         Councilman Dirk Fulton
                         City of Benicia
                         250 East L Street
                         Benicia, CA 94510

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

                         Very truly yours,

                         [signature]
                         Shawn M. Mason
                         City Attorney

smm/kz
F/FPPC.FUL
Attachment
cc:  Councilman Dirk Fulton

sections.question: Whether Councilman Dirk Fulton may participate in any or all of the planning actions the City Council will take on the proposed Sky Valley Specific Plan, given his property interests in the area.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Councilman Fulton, who owns a 1.82-acre parcel located over one mile from the Sky Valley Plan area, participate in City Council decisions regarding the adoption and implementation of the Specific Plan without violating the Fair Political Practices Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or short answer section present. The request seeks advice on potential conflicts of interest under the Fair Political Practices Act. Document is from City Attorney (requester) to FPPC, dated June 8, 1992.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Councilman Dirk Fulton may participate in any or all of the planning actions the City Council will take on the proposed Sky Valley Specific Plan, given his property interests in the area.

embedding.summary: City Attorney for Benicia requests written advice from FPPC on behalf of Councilman Dirk Fulton regarding whether he may participate in City Council actions on the Sky Valley Specific Plan, which involves developing 5,000 acres into a residential community, given that Fulton owns property that could potentially be affected by the plan's infrastructure improvements.
